data.title: As a developer, using-lockpick guides Claude to invoke brainstorm when feature intent is detected
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add explicit feature-intent detection guidance to using-lockpick SKILL.md and HOOK-INJECTION.md with concrete signal patterns for when brainstorm should be invoked.
**Why**: The soft enforcement layer — Claude needs clear guidance to recognize feature-intent signals and route to brainstorm proactively, before the hard gate (PreToolUse hook) blocks.
**Scope**:
- IN: Feature-intent detection section in SKILL.md, matching guidance in HOOK-INJECTION.md, explicit and implicit signal patterns
- OUT: Sub-agent routing guidance (owned by dso-khl2), PreToolUse hook mechanism (Story 1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, SKILL.md includes a Feature Intent Detection section with explicit signal patterns (explicit: "new feature", "create an epic", "I want to build"; implicit: proposals for significant new capabilities or architectural changes)
  ← Satisfies: "The using-lockpick skill includes explicit feature-intent detection guidance"
- When this story is complete, HOOK-INJECTION.md includes matching feature-intent detection guidance
  ← Satisfies: "both SKILL.md and HOOK-INJECTION.md"
- When this story is complete, guidance is scoped to brainstorm-enforcement only (sub-agent routing guidance owned by dso-khl2)
  ← Satisfies: "scoped to brainstorm-enforcement guidance only"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Scope] The using-lockpick SKILL.md already contains EnterPlanMode brainstorm enforcement in its flowchart (lines 25-38: "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke /dso:brainstorm skill"). This story must reconcile with this existing content — enhance it with concrete signal patterns rather than adding duplicate guidance
